folio.label: 67r
folio.l: , Incipit ¸logus vxoris de Bathe

note.notetxt: 1It begins in the same folio as ML finishes. The text is copied in a lighter shade of the usual brown. It is different from the on ein CO. The colour of the paper of this folio is yellowish, very dirty, worne dout. It may have an impact on the colour of the ink. ODR

folio.l: Ex¹ience , thougú noon auctoritee
Were in this world , is rigút I now for me
To speken of woo , that is in mariage
For lordynges , syn I twelue ©er was of age
Thanked be god , that is eterne on lyue
Husbondes at the chirche doore , haue I had fyue
If I so often mygút , han wedded be
And alle weren worthy men , in here degree
But me was told , nougút longe agon is
That sithen Crist , ne went neuäe but oonys
To weddyng , in the Cane of Galilee
That by the same ensaumple , taugút he me
That I ne wedded , shuld be but ones
Loo ; herkene whicú , a sharp~ word , for the nones

note.notetxt: 14;: top stroke of punctus elevatus visible in MS, bottom point not visible. Check the manuscript/ODR/

folio.l: Beside a welle , iúc god and man
Spak in repreue , of the Samaritan
Thow hast I had , fyue husbondes q¶ he
And that ilke man , whicú that now hatú the
Is nat thyn husbonde , thus seide he certeyn
What that he ment ther by , I can nougút seyn
But that I aske why , that the fifte man
Was noon husbonde , to the Samaritan
How many mygút she haue , in mariage
ñet herd I neuäe , tellen in myn age
Vp on this noumbre , diffiniciou~
Men may deuyne , and glosen vp and doun~
But wel I woot , expresse , witú outen lye
God bad vs for to wexe , and multiplie
